response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo demonstrates strong software development skills and versatility across multiple domains, but her experience lacks direct alignment with the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect role, particularly in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, and hardware-software co-design. Her background in full-stack development, AI-agent infrastructure, and open-source GIS projects is impressive but tangential to the specialized hardware and performance analysis focus of this role.
strengths: Extensive experience in software development across diverse domains (web, mobile, embedded, and AI-agent infrastructure).
Strong open-source contributions and technical leadership, including workshops and talks on advanced topics.
Proven ability to work with high-performance systems (e.g., low-latency apps in DLang, real-time metadata handling).
concerns: No direct experience with SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, or hardware-software co-design.
Lacks explicit mention of SIMD/MIMD architectures, GPU workload analysis, or deep-learning performance bottlenecks.
Educational background (Bachelor's in CS) meets the minimum requirement but does not include advanced degrees or specialized training in hardware performance.
